text:
New papers published about Machine Learning for TPV in ACS Photonics and probe cleaning methods for AFM in IOP Publishing.
pubs.acs.org/doi/full/10.... iopscience.iop.org/article/10.1...
Thanks to UC Davis, for a nice story about our recent TPV work: "Researchers are Creating a Brighter Future for Solar Cells — Without the Sun" ece.ucdavis.edu/news/researc...

We are happy to share our latest work using Machine Learning and AI for High-Efficiency Thermophotovoltaics published in ACS_AMI.bsky.social
pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/...

Our team's work on the Casimir effect is featured in the newest issue of Engineering Progress magazine ucdavisengineering.bsky.social Story: engineering.ucdavis.edu/engineering-... Magazine: engineering.ucdavis.edu/engineering-...

Our work on a passive radiative cooling engine has been highlighted across several media. Thanks for your interesting and support!
physicsworld.com/a/heat-engin... spectrum.ieee.org/radiative-co... scitechdaily.com/new-engine-t... www.techeblog.com/uc-davis-mod...

New article in SPIE's Nov-Dec Photonics Focus entitled "Cooling without a fan," features our work and discusses how radiative cooling via photonics technologies could help the environment.
spie.org/news/photoni...

Now introducing... The Technically Hip! 🎸🎤 Check out our “rockumentary” to see the unexpected side of the ucdavis College of Engineering band, who teach, research and inspire the next generation of engineers. 🎶 Meet this truly “Hip” ensemble: www.ucdavis.edu/magazine/uc-...

Prof. Jeremy Munday is interviewed on the Science Podcast about their recent work in Science Advances.
www.science.org/content/podc...

Check out our new paper featured on the cover of Science Advances about mechanical power generation using passive radiative cooling.
www.science.org/doi/10.1126/...
